Analiza Spotify a zdrowie psychiczne

Michał Błoch

2026-01-23

Wczytanie danych

setwd("/Users/michalbloch/Downloads")
mxmh <- read.csv("mxmh_survey_results.csv", check.names=FALSE)
spotify <- bind_rows(
  read.csv("high_popularity_spotify_data.csv"),
  read.csv("low_popularity_spotify_data.csv")
)

Przygotowanie danych ankiety

genre_cols <- c(
  "Frequency [Classical]","Frequency [Country]","Frequency [EDM]",
  "Frequency [Folk]","Frequency [Gospel]","Frequency [Hip hop]",
  "Frequency [Jazz]","Frequency [K pop]","Frequency [Latin]",
  "Frequency [Lofi]","Frequency [Metal]","Frequency [Pop]",
  "Frequency [R&B]","Frequency [Rap]","Frequency [Rock]",
  "Frequency [Video game music]"
)
freq_map <- c("Never"=0,"Rarely"=1,"Sometimes"=2,"Very frequently"=3)

mxmh_num <- mxmh %>%
  select(`Fav genre`, Anxiety, Depression, Insomnia, OCD, all_of(genre_cols)) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(genre_cols), ~ freq_map[.x]))

genre_mapping <- c(
  "Frequency [Classical]"="classical",
  "Frequency [Country]"="country",
  "Frequency [EDM]"="edm",
  "Frequency [Folk]"="folk",
  "Frequency [Gospel]"="gospel",
  "Frequency [Hip hop]"="hip hop",
  "Frequency [Jazz]"="jazz",
  "Frequency [K pop]"="k pop",
  "Frequency [Latin]"="latin",
  "Frequency [Lofi]"="lofi",
  "Frequency [Metal]"="metal",
  "Frequency [Pop]"="pop",
  "Frequency [R&B]"="r&b",
  "Frequency [Rap]"="rap",
  "Frequency [Rock]"="rock",
  "Frequency [Video game music]"="video game music"
)

mxmh_long <- mxmh_num %>%
  pivot_longer(cols=all_of(genre_cols), names_to="genre", values_to="frequency") %>%
  mutate(genre = tolower(genre_mapping[genre])) %>%
  filter(frequency>0)

Przygotowanie danych Spotify

audio_cols <- c("energy","valence","tempo","danceability",
                "loudness","acousticness","instrumentalness","speechiness")

audio_scaled <- spotify %>%
  select(playlist_genre, all_of(audio_cols)) %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  mutate(across(-playlist_genre, scale)) %>%
  mutate(playlist_genre = tolower(trimws(playlist_genre)))

joined_test <- mxmh_long %>%
  left_join(audio_scaled, by=c("genre"="playlist_genre"))
## Warning in left_join(., audio_scaled, by = c(genre = "playlist_genre")): Detected an unexpected many-to-many relationship between `x` and `y`.
## ℹ Row 1 of `x` matches multiple rows in `y`.
## ℹ Row 140 of `y` matches multiple rows in `x`.
## ℹ If a many-to-many relationship is expected, set `relationship =
##   "many-to-many"` to silence this warning.
joined_test_clean <- joined_test %>%
  drop_na(all_of(audio_cols))

Eksploracja danych

health_vars <- c("Anxiety","Depression","Insomnia","OCD")

summary(joined_test_clean)

sapply(joined_test_clean, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))
##        Fav genre          Anxiety       Depression         Insomnia 
##                0                0                0                0 
##              OCD            genre        frequency           energy 
##                0                0                0                0 
##          valence            tempo     danceability         loudness 
##                0                0                0                0 
##     acousticness instrumentalness      speechiness 
##                0                0                0

Histogramy

for(var in health_vars){
  p <- ggplot(joined_test_clean, aes_string(x=var)) +
    geom_histogram(bins=10, fill="skyblue", color="black") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels=comma) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title=paste("Histogram:",var), x=var, y="Liczba obserwacji")
  print(p)
}
## Warning: `aes_string()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.0.0.
## ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`.
## ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

Boxploty

for(col in audio_cols){
  p <- ggplot(joined_test_clean, aes_string(y=col)) +
    geom_boxplot(fill="salmon") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title=paste("Boxplot:",col), y=col)
  print(p)
}

Korelacja Spearmana

test_spearman_verbose <- function(df, health_var){
  problem_cols <- audio_cols[sapply(df[audio_cols], function(x) all(is.na(x)))]
  valid_cols <- setdiff(audio_cols, problem_cols)
  if(length(valid_cols)<1) return(NULL)
  
  df_clean <- df %>%
    select(all_of(c(valid_cols, health_var))) %>%
    filter(complete.cases(.))
  if(nrow(df_clean)<2) return(NULL)
  
  cor_values <- sapply(valid_cols, function(col){
    cor(df_clean[[col]], df_clean[[health_var]], method="spearman")
  })
  data.frame(feature=valid_cols, correlation=cor_values)
}
plot_health_correlation <- function(df, health_var){
  cor_df <- test_spearman_verbose(df, health_var)
  if(is.null(cor_df)) return(NULL)
  
  max_abs <- max(abs(cor_df$correlation), na.rm=TRUE)
  
  p <- ggplot(cor_df, aes(x=feature, y=correlation, fill=correlation)) +
    geom_col() +
    coord_flip() +
    theme_minimal() +
    scale_y_continuous(limits=c(-max_abs,max_abs), expand=c(0,0)) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low="darkblue", mid="white", high="dodgerblue", midpoint=0) +
    labs(title=paste("Spearman korelacje cech audio z poziomem", health_var),
         x="Cecha audio", y="Spearman rho")
  print(p)
}

for(var in health_vars){
  plot_health_correlation(joined_test_clean, var)
}

Regresja liniowa

linear_models <- list()
coef_tables <- list()
coef_plots <- list()

for(var in health_vars){
  formula <- as.formula(paste(var,"~",paste(audio_cols,collapse=" + ")))
  model <- lm(formula, data=joined_test_clean)
  linear_models[[var]] <- model
  
  coef_df <- broom::tidy(model) %>%
    filter(term!="(Intercept)") %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(estimate)))
  coef_tables[[var]] <- coef_df
  
  max_abs <- max(abs(coef_df$estimate))
  
  p <- ggplot(coef_df, aes(x=reorder(term,estimate), y=estimate, fill=estimate)) +
    geom_col() +
    coord_flip() +
    theme_minimal() +
    scale_y_continuous(limits=c(-max_abs,max_abs), expand=c(0,0)) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low="darkred", mid="white", high="darkgreen", midpoint=0) +
    labs(title=paste("Wpływ cech audio na",var),
         x="Cecha audio", y="Współczynnik regresji")
  print(p)
  coef_plots[[var]] <- p
}
